clinical_history: What was the cause of the tall right precordial R waves in this 32-year-old woman?
diagnosis: b) The limb leads are normal, so dextrocardia is not  present.  But the chest leads were inadvertently reversed:  Lead V6=V1, V5=V2, etc. This artifact may simulate posterior or posterolateral myocardial infarction (MI ) (see Case #56) or RVH (see Case # 51). The degree of Wolff-Parkinson-White pre-excitation is excluded by the absence of the triad of short PR interval, wide QRS and delta wave (see Case # 184).
